Select which components are installed in: Select which components are installed in your Sigenergy system.
Modbus address of the EVAC charger: Modbus address of the EVAC charger
How often to read Modbus registers. Mini: How often to read Modbus registers. Minimum: 5000 ms
Connection Test: sends the current setti: Connection Test: sends the current settings to the adapter and attempts to read register 30000 (plant system time) via Modbus.
Select which additional statistics shoul: Select which additional statistics should be calculated and written as ioBroker states. These values are derived from live Modbus measurements.
How long until the battery reaches 100%: How long until the battery reaches 100% at current charge rate
How long the battery will last at curren: How long the battery will last at current consumption
Today: how long it took until the batter: Today: how long it took until the battery was fully charged
How long today the battery covered the h: How long today the battery covered the house consumption without grid
Percentage of PV production used locally: Percentage of PV production used locally (not exported)
Percentage of house consumption covered: Percentage of house consumption covered by PV + battery
Average PV power output over the last 15: Average PV power output over the last 15 minutes
Track daily grid export and import energ: Track daily grid export and import energy
Always calculated (cannot be disabled):: Always calculated (cannot be disabled): House Power (PV + Battery discharge − Grid export) · Today max/min SOC · Formatted durations (e.g. "2h 15m")
Sigenergy SigenMicro module-level power: Sigenergy SigenMicro module-level power electronics (MLPE). Activates the SigenMicro scan & configuration tab.
Lowest slave address to probe during the: Lowest slave address to probe during the SigenMicro device scan (1–246)
Highest slave address to probe during th: Highest slave address to probe during the SigenMicro device scan (1–246)
Configure the Modbus slave-ID range to s: Configure the Modbus slave-ID range to scan for SigenMicro micro-inverters. After scanning, enable each discovered device individually. The adapter polls all enabled devices in every cycle.
Click the button below to scan the Modbu: Click the button below to scan the Modbus bus for SigenMicro devices. The scan uses the ID range configured above. Found devices are saved automatically — use the SigenMicro admin tab to enable or disable individual devices.
Use the SigenMicro tab in the adapter ad: Use the SigenMicro tab in the adapter admin page to run the scan and manage discovered devices. The device list and individual enable/disable selections are stored automatically.
Scan progress (live — updates automatica: Scan progress (live — updates automatically during scan):
Live scan progress (updates while scan i: Live scan progress (updates while scan is running):
Found devices (stored after scan — use t: Found devices (stored after scan — use the adapter config to enable/disable individual devices):
Scan the Modbus bus for SigenMicro micro: Scan the Modbus bus for SigenMicro micro-inverters. The scan uses the existing Modbus connection and pauses polling temporarily. IDs used by Plant, Inverter, and Chargers are skipped automatically.
